rulings.Case Title: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, IN THEIR CAPACITIES AS et al VS. ADVANCED INSTALLATION AND MARKO VLADISLAVICH, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, Line 3. PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, IN THEIR CAPACITIES AS, CARPENTERS PENSION TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, CARPENTERS ANNUITY TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, CARPENTERS TRAINING TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN, CARPENTERS 46 NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COUNTIES' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to corporate Respondent, Advanced Installation. Denied without prejudice as to individual Respondent, Vladislavich. The record shows that Vladislavich was not a party to the 2009 Arbitration Award or the 2013 Amended Arbitration Award. (Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award Exhibits B, C.) Once the arbitration goes to hearing against the corporate defendant only, an individual not made a party to the arbitration is not bound by it absent special circumstances. (Southern Cal. Pipe Trades Dist. Council No. 16 v. Merritt (1981) 126 Cal.App.3d 530, 536; see also Motores De Mexicali, S. A. v. Superior Court In and For Los Angeles County (1958) 51 Cal.2d 172, 175-176.) Petitioners' moving papers do not show special circumstances exist. Further, CCP § 1285 must be applied in a manner consistent with due process of law. (Merritt, supra, 126 Cal.App.3d at 539.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LISA HABBESHAW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT HONORWAY INVESTMENT CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel physical examination is granted. The record reflects it was defendant's examiner, Dr. Cassini, not defense counsel, who cancelled the examination. The doctor concedes his failure to understand/oversight as to the agreed-by-counsel examination ground rules and as a result failed to raise his examination self-recording practice to the party who retained him. The Court cannot find a waiver of the defendant's statutory right to a physical examination based on an admitted error by a non-party. On that basis and the additional factor of the complex injuries claimed in the action there is good cause for setting a second physical examination pursuant to CCP 2032.310. The defendant shall serve a written demand for said examination. Plaintiff shall respond with an agreement to comply or modify. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested or supply same in digital format to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDZEJ PACZYNSKI VS. CHRIS JAMES CO. DBA VANGUARD PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, Line 11. DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION, PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Listing Agreement states that the parties agree to mediate prior to commencing suit or arbitration; however, if a party fails to do so, that party is not entitled to recover attorney's fees. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), Ex. A.) Therefore, the contract provides that the remedy for failure to comply with this contractual provision is loss of attorney's fees, not barring the claims. Defendants have not established that Robert Carter is an indispensable party to the suit. Although Mr. Carter executed the Listing Agreement, he executed the contract as an agent on behalf of a principal, Vanguard Properties. When an agent executes a contract on behalf of a disclosed principal, the presumption is that the principal and not the agent is to be bound. (Bank of America Nat. Trust and Savings Ass'n v. Cryer (1936) 6 Cal.2d 485, 489.) Because the FAC seeks relief from Vanguard Properties, as the principal, and not from Mr. Carter, as the agent, Mr. Carter is not an indispensable party. Persons against whom no relief is sought cannot properly be joined as defendants. (Pinnacle Holdings Inc. v. Simon (1995) 31 Cal.Appl.4th 1430, 1437.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDZEJ PACZYNSKI VS. CHRIS JAMES CO. DBA VANGUARD PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, Line 11. DEFENDANTS JUILETTE VO, CHIS JAMES CORPORATION, PAULA GOLD-NOCELLA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Ev. Code sec. 452(h). The following portions of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") are stricken pursuant to CCP sec. 436(a): par. 13, pg. 5, lines 22-24; par. 18, pg. 7, lines 8-14; par. 1, pg. 1, line 27 - pg. 2, line 2; par. 13, pg. 5, line 28 - pg. 6, line 1; par. 18, pg. 7, lines 6-7. The FAC does not plead a statutory or contractual basis for attorney's fees. Additionally, the opposition to the motion to strike did not set forth any argument explaining why the tort of another doctrine applies. The FAC does not plead facts establishing Defendants acted with "oppression, fraud, or malice" to support a prayer for punitive damages. (Civ. Code sec. 3294(a); Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) The Court takes judicial notice pursuant to Ev. Code sec. 452(h) of the fact that Vanguard Properties was a licensed corporation throughout the relevant time period alleged in the complaint and therefore strikes the allegations it was unlicensed pursuant to CCP sec. 436(a). The opposition papers do not address striking the allegations pertaining to Ms. Vo. Therefore, the motion to strike those portions of the FAC is granted as unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GINA CUNNINGHAM VS. AUCTION.COM, INC., et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014, Line 15. DEFENDANT AUCTION.COM, LLC'S Motion To Transfer Venue. Granted. The case is transferred to San Mateo County. Govt. Code sec. 12965(b) provides that venue is proper in the county where: (1) the discriminatory conduct is alleged to have occurred; (2) the relevant records are maintained and administered; or (3) where the aggrieved individuals would have worked but for the alleged discriminatory acts. It is undisputed that under the first two prongs venue is not proper in San Francisco County. As to the third prong, the focus is on where the employee would have worked but for the alleged discrimination. Here, it is undisputed that (1) Defendant does not have an office in San Francisco County (Declaration of Sheryl Roland, 3); (2) Plaintiff was hired to work in the San Mateo County office (Id., 5); (3) Plaintiff was terminated out of the San Mateo County office (Id., 7); and (4) Defendant had a policy requiring all employees to seek approval to work from home on a regular basis and Plaintiff never submitted such a request (Id., 6). These facts establish that Plaintiff would have worked out of the San Mateo County office but for the alleged discriminatory acts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
